event_detail: First location opens Oct 29, 1982 — "On The Border South Texas Café"
Acquired for undisclosed sum; 21 units at time of sale; franchise program follows
Brinker begins franchising OTB — accelerates national expansion
Chain crosses 100 US locations under Brinker ownership
Cinco de Mayo sets all-time single-day sales record; 8 new locations added
All-time high location count; casual dining still dominant
Fast casual growth accelerates; OTB sales and unit count begin falling

event_label: Brinker sells OTB to Golden Gate
event_detail: $180M sale to Golden Gate Capital PE; Brinker refocuses on Chili's
NPD Group — fast casual growing at 7% CAGR; casual dining losing share

event_label: Golden Gate sells to Argonne Capital
event_detail: Second PE sale in 4 years; Argonne also owns Applebee's/IHOP franchises
Jan 2014 — 1st Assistant Manager, Alpharetta GA; Argonne acquisition same month
$3 meltdown margarita push; tortilla station chronically unstaffed; FOH stretched
Jan 2016 departure; Alpharetta location subsequently closed — suburban market exit
Technomic data — decade of decline confirmed; never recovered to Brinker-era levels
Dining room closures devastate full-service casual dining; OTB drops to ~100 locations
Locations tick back up to 125; sales recover partially but not to pre-COVID levels
Technomic: OTB still below pre-COVID volumes; inflation and traffic decline continue
March 5, 2025 — $19.6M debt; 77 locations closed pre-filing; blames inflation/labor
May 2025 — Houston-based Pappas wins auction; ~55 surviving locations; brand continues
